license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ybdfsi6
title: Isolation of endothelial cells from umbillical vessels
author: Thomas Mudersbach

steps_raw.instruction: Hank's Balanced Salt Solution (HBSS) (H6648-500ml, Sigma-Aldrich) Enzymatic digestion with Dispase II (29585300, Roche) (2.4 U/ml) in Ham's F12 Ham's F12 with 10% fetal calf serum (FCS), penicillin (50 U/ml) and streptomycin (50 µg/ml) for centrifugation Endothelial cell culture medium: MCDB131 (10372-019, Gibco) containing 10% FCS, L-glutamine (10mM), penicillin (50 U/ml), streptomycin (50 µg/ml), epidermal growth factor (0.1 ng/ml), basic fibroblast growth factor (1 ng/ml) and endothelial growth supplement with Heparin (0.4%)

umbilical cord is cut at one end (about 1 cm) to identify the arteries and the vein the vein or artery is then cannulated with a button cannula connected to a three-way stop cock. The cannula is fixed with a cable connector to the cord The cord is the rinsed with HBSS until the HBSS remains clear and no blood is washed out any longer Repeat steps 1-3 on the other end Now both ends are cannulated and the vessel is filled with Dispase II solution The cord is incubated for 00:50:00, min at 37 °C Discard the Dispase II solution and replace with Ham's F12 containing 10 % FCS Carefully massage the cord to loosen the endothelial cells Collect the medium in a suitable centrifugation tube and centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 100 x g Discard the supernatant and resuspend pellet in endothelial cell culture medium and distribute on a fibronectin-coated cell culture dish. Coating with fibronectin should be performed 00:30:00, min to 01:00:00, min before use. Place the cell culture dish containing your cells in a cell incubator at 37 °C in 5% CO 2 . After 04:00:00, hr cells are washed with cell culture medium. 48:00:00, hr to 96:00:00, hr after isolation the cells should have formed a confluent cell layer
